description: Telegram Clean UI - Fix missing content (diffs, truncated responses, lost plans) and replace noisy per-tool messages with condensed activity digests

userStories.title: Build formatActivityDigest() helper in telegram-format.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a formatting function that converts a list of buffered tool events into a single condensed digest line, so the digest logic is testable and reusable.
acceptanceCriteria: New exported function formatActivityDigest() in server/telegram-format.ts
Input: array of objects with { tool: string, toolInput: any, assistantText?: string } shape
Output format: '⚙️ verb1 · verb2 · verb3' as HTML-escaped string
Deduplicates repeated tools: 5 Reads become 'read 5 files', 3 Greps become '3 searches'
Edit/Write tools always include basename: 'edited foo.ts' not 'edited file'
Bash tool uses toolInput.description when available, otherwise truncated command (max 40 chars)
Mixed example output: '⚙️ edited foo.ts · ran tests · read 3 files'
Returns empty string when input array is empty or contains no meaningful tool events
When assistantText is present in events, picks the longest non-trivial one (skip single sentences starting with 'Let me', 'I\'ll', 'Now') and appends as second line: '\n {text max 300 chars}'
Reasoning text cleaned of <!--rc:...--> markers before display
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Look at existing formatEventLine() in telegram-format.ts for verb/icon patterns. Reuse its logic internally but produce a collapsed single-line output instead of per-tool lines. Use escapeHtml() (already in file) for all user-facing text.

userStories.title: Fix response truncation in finished notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want to read Claude's full response in the finished notification instead of a 3.5KB truncation of a 16KB response.
acceptanceCriteria: In server/TelegramBot.ts onStatusChange handler, remove the .slice(0, 3500) truncation on session.lastAssistantText
Instead, allow up to 8000 chars of response text
If response exceeds 8000 chars, truncate at 8000 with '[... truncated, N chars total]' suffix
Use splitMessage() (already exists in telegram-format.ts) to split the formatted HTML across multiple Telegram messages if it exceeds 4000 chars
Send each split part as a separate sendMessage call to the same topic
Expandable blockquote still used for content over 300 chars
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The truncation is in TelegramBot.ts in the onStatusChange method where it does session.lastAssistantText?.slice(0, 3500). splitMessage() is already exported from telegram-format.ts and handles 4000 char Telegram limit. The sendMessage method on TelegramBot already exists.

userStories.title: Persist plan content through to finished notification
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the plan Claude executed in the finished message, so I have a record of what was planned.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional planContent field to session object in server/SessionManager.ts (on the Session interface or inline type)
When a pre_tool_use event for ExitPlanMode is processed, store toolInput.planContent on the session
Clear planContent when session transitions to 'working' status (new task started)
In TelegramBot.ts onStatusChange for idle: if session.planContent exists, include it in the finished notification
Plan shown with '📋 Plan:' header before the response summary, formatted with markdownToTelegramHtml()
Plan content budget: max 3000 chars (truncate with '[... plan truncated]' if longer)
Also add planContent to shared/types.ts Session interface if it exists there
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ExitPlanMode events already have planContent in toolInput (hook.sh reads the plan file and adds it). The session object is defined either in shared/types.ts or server/SessionManager.ts. Check both.

userStories.title: Replace per-tool event stream with activity digests
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool calls collapsed into periodic digest messages instead of one message per tool call.
acceptanceCriteria: In TelegramBot.ts onEvent method, tool events (pre_tool_use) are buffered with their tool name, toolInput, and assistantText
flushEventBuffer() calls formatActivityDigest() on the buffered tool events to produce one digest line instead of joining individual formatEventLine() outputs
Stop events with non-silent markers still generate their own standalone messages (not collapsed into digest)
ExitPlanMode pre_tool_use events bypass the digest buffer and generate an immediate standalone plan message
user_prompt_submit events still tracked for initialPrompt but NOT included in digest output
Digest messages sent with disable_notification: true (no push notification sound)
If formatActivityDigest returns empty string, no message is sent for that flush cycle
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing eventBuffers Map in TelegramBot.ts already groups events per session with a 10s flush interval. Adapt the buffer format and flush logic rather than rebuilding. The buffer currently stores formatted strings — change it to store raw event data so formatActivityDigest can process them. Look at how formatEventLine() is currently called in onEvent and replace that path.

userStories.title: Show edit diffs in permission prompts
userStories.description: As a user reviewing permission prompts for Edit tools, I want to see what code is being changed, not just the filename.
acceptanceCriteria: In formatSessionWaiting() in telegram-format.ts, add special handling for Edit tool (similar to existing ExitPlanMode handling)
When tool is Edit and toolInput has old_string and new_string, show a diff preview
Diff format: show old_string (prefixed with - lines) and new_string (prefixed with + lines) inside <pre> tags
Limit diff preview to first 15 lines total (combined old + new), truncate with '[... N more lines]'
Total diff budget: max 1500 chars to fit within Telegram message alongside other content
For Write tool, show filename + first 5 lines of content in <pre> block
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: formatSessionWaiting() already has special handling for ExitPlanMode that shows planContent in an expandable blockquote. Follow the same pattern for Edit. toolInput has file_path, old_string, new_string fields.

userStories.title: Clean up finished notification format
userStories.description: As a user, I want the finished notification to be concise — short summaries inline, long ones in blockquotes.
acceptanceCriteria: Update formatSessionFinished() in telegram-format.ts
Short summaries (<150 chars): inline format '✅ name finished — summary here' (single line, no blockquote)
Medium summaries (150-500 chars): header line + summary as indented text below (no expandable blockquote)
Long summaries (500+ chars): header + expandable blockquote (current behavior preserved)
RC marker message used as summary when available (markerMsg parameter), falling back to snippet
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: formatSessionFinished() is in server/telegram-format.ts. Currently always uses expandable blockquote for any content. The markerMsg comes from the <!--rc:category:message--> markers that Claude adds to responses.

userStories.title: Suppress push notifications for activity digests
userStories.description: As a user, I only want my phone to buzz for questions and finished status, not for every activity digest.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity digest messages from flushEventBuffer sent with disable_notification: true
Finished notifications (idle status) sent WITH push notification (current behavior)
Question/approval notifications (waiting status) sent WITH push notification (current behavior)
Plan messages (ExitPlanMode standalone) sent WITH push notification
Verify that the sendMessage method in TelegramBot supports passing disable_notification option through to the Telegram API
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The grammY bot.api.sendMessage already supports disable_notification as an option. Check how the existing sendMessage wrapper in TelegramBot.ts passes options through — may need to add a parameter for silent mode. Event buffer messages currently use silent: true which maps to disable_notification.
